zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Edward Higgins於1545年9月7日出生在英國賀福爾萊奧明斯特時，他的父親Reverend John Higgins - Knight17歲，母親Mary Elizabeth Clynton22歲。他於1598年與Julian Elizabeth Meals在英國賀福爾萊奧明斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1631年3月14日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾阿波茲 明日香享年85歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾賀特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Higgins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hUiginn ‘descendant of Uiginn’, a byname meaning ‘viking, sea-rover’ (from Old Norse víkingr). Compare McGuigan .

English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Higgin, itself either a Lancashire variant of Huggin (from a pet form of Hugh ), or a variant of Hickin (from a pet form of Richard ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. See Hick and compare Higson .

variants.name: Higgens
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Higgins  .

variants.name: McGuigan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam, Mairead.
Irish (northern): perhaps an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Uiginn ‘son of the viking’ (see Higgins 1).
Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Guagáin, a shortened form of Mag Eochagáin, son of Eochagán, a diminutive of Eochaidh ‘horseman’. Compare Geoghegan .

variants.name: Haggins
meanings: Scottish variant of Irish   Higgins  .

variants.name: Heggins
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Higgins   1.

variants.name: Kiggins
meanings: Irish (Galway and Mayo) and Manx: shortened Anglicized form (with English excrescent -s) of Gaelic Mag Uiginn ‘son of the Viking’ (see Higgins ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Edward Higgins was born on 7 September 1545, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, his father, Reverend John Higgins - Knight, was 17 and his mother, Mary Elizabeth Clynton, was 22. He married Julian Elizabeth Meals in 1598, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 14 March 1631, in Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85, and was buried in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Edward Higgins est né le 7 septembre 1545, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Reverend John Higgins - Knight, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Mary Elizabeth Clynton, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Julian Elizabeth Meals en 1598, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 14 mars 1631, à Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Edward Higgins am 7. September 1545 in Leominster, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Reverend John Higgins - Knight 17 und seine Mutter Mary Elizabeth Clynton 22. Er hat 1598 in Leominster, Herefordshire, England Julian Elizabeth Meals geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 14. März 1631 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Julian Elizabeth Meals nel 1598, nel Leominster, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Richard Edward Higginsが1545年9月7日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアレオミンスターで生まれた時，父Reverend John Higgins - Knightは17歳，母Mary Elizabeth Clyntonは22歳であった。1598年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアレオミンスターでJulian Elizabeth Mealsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1631年3月14日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアアボツ ラングリーで85歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Edward Higgins(이)가 1545년 9월 7일 영국 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Reverend John Higgins - Knight(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Elizabeth Clynton(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1598년 영국 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 Julian Elizabeth Meals(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1631년 3월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 애벗스 랭글리에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Edward Higgins nasceu em 7 de setembro de 1545, em Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Reverend John Higgins - Knight, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Mary Elizabeth Clynton, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Julian Elizabeth Meals em 1598, em Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 14 de março de 1631, em Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Edward Higgins родился 7 сентября 1545 г. в местности леоминстир, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Reverend John Higgins - Knight было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Elizabeth Clynton, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Julian Elizabeth Meals в 1598 г. в местности леоминстир, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 14 марта 1631 г. в местности эббоц лангли, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England,: Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Edward Higgins nació el 7 de septiembre de 1545, en Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Reverend John Higgins - Knight, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Mary Elizabeth Clynton, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Julian Elizabeth Meals en 1598, en Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 14 de marzo de 1631, en Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
